title: Crocus’s Life Inside Laboon
description: On pages 1–4, crocus’s life inside laboon as crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line.
scene: Crocus’s Life Inside Laboon. Crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: This water way is very good.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: He has a big hole inside his body, but he's still alive.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Isn't this because he is playful?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's just my funny idea, but I am a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: When I used to stay at Mizaki, I worked on a ship for 2-3 years.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: A doc- Tor?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Can you become our doctor, then?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Don't be stupid

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I am not as strong as I used to be now.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You are a doctor, so, you are giving this whole medical treatment from inside his body, right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Yes. Because he's getting too big for outside treatment now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Here I open the door for you.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ha!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I see the real sky!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What are we going to do with these two?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just throw them out somewhere around here.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What's this?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Is this gas- Tric Li- Quid?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No. It's sea water, Ms. Wednesday.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It seems that those pirates

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: are letting us go, Mr. Nine.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So, who are you?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's none of your business.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wait, Ms. Wednesday. Even though it's none of his business, we still have some relationship with pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's right, Mr. Nine. It really involves our company. You'd better.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Be pre- par- ed!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We will see you again soon. You countryside pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Claucus! One day, we will take that whale for sure!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Her name is Ms. Wednesday? Wow! What a mysterious girl she is.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: whose is this?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Is it a good idea to let them get away? They can come back to kill the whale.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If we hold them here, they will send new guys to kill the whale anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: But it's been 50 years.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: He still believes that his friends will return?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Those pirates made this whale wait for them for a really long time.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Idiot! Do you remember this is Grand Line?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: They said that they'll come back in 2-3 years,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: But it's already been 50 years. He should know an answer.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: They are already dead!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: No matter how long he wait for them, they will never come back.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on crocus’s life inside laboon. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
